title: Angels The Child Interpretation
wikitext:
[]: Angels: The Child Interpretation             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 7:50 pm A theory that makes Seele look like even bigger bastards than they already are. I shouldn't really call this a theory, since it's more just my ramblings and I am certain others have thought of this before, but w/e. First of all, we know that the Angels were forced out of Adam's Chamber of Guf thanks to the actions of Seele. They tried to implant human DNA into Adam, which didn't end too well. Their use of the LoL prevented the Angels from emerging immediately and it prevented wholesale annihilation, but it still left the world half destroyed. When Adam was reduced to an embryo state, the angels were tossed into the world of humanity without much warning. They are like children who don't know what's going on. All they know is that these big bad humans are keeping mommy away from them, and they want to go back to mommy. And when those big bad humans try to hurt them, the Angels fight back. The Angels never attack random civilians. They only target people who attack them, or those who they believe are holding Adam captive. They never go on a rampage throughout New York, killing innocents just for the lulz. Who's to say that they know that the consequences of their reuniting with Adam would lead to the destruction of humanity? A child doesn't understand that certain actions they may take may have dire consequences. In addition, the angels are invariably alone. They never attack together, it's possible they don't even know about each other's existence. They will even settle joining with an Eva unit. One might call their mindrape behavior slightly strange, but one could look at it as just playing with a new toy. They want to find out what's inside the minds of those little people who are trying to kill them. Please feel free to tear this little post apart and offer opinons. As for me, I'm off to work on a speech for a communications class.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:06 pm I always relish having more people in the "Angels are children" club. (I myself have ranted extensively about the subject.) Another point that makes this interpretation so much more potent for me is that the Angels are being killed not only by entities created in their mother's image*, but by fellow children (the Eva pilots). Poor Sandalphon... *(Even if one disagrees about Eva-01 being genetically Adam or whatever, she looks just as much like Adam as the other Evas.)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:07 pm This theory has been discussed before by a few users, yes. I don't really see the fascination with the idea, myself. Angels are far more interesting when their motivations are that of unknowable alien god-beings rather than that of participants in a giant-scale reenaction of P.D. Eastman's Are You My Mother? .

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:19 pm Wait, I thought this was canon?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:22 pm @essel: I'm probably just repeating myself from the last time you replied using the "Are You My Mother?" thing -- but it is more than possible for the Angels to be children yearning for their mother (which is one of those really heavily implied thematic things, anyway, c.f. Kaworu in 24) AND entities whose existences cannot possibly be fully comprehended by our puny, frail Lilin-ness. It isn't as though they're mutually exclusive. (By the way, if you're using "alien" in the sense of "extraterrestrial", the Angels aren't it.)

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:57 pm esselfortium wrote: This theory has been discussed before by a few users, yes. I don't really see the fascination with the idea, myself. Angels are far more interesting when their motivations are that of unknowable alien god-beings rather than that of participants in a giant-scale reenaction of P.D. Eastman's Are You My Mother? .

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:15 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:18 pm Rewatch the series, and watch the Angels closely while ignoring what the Lilin say about them.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:19 pm The giant monsters that parachute themselves onto cities as giant bombs, infect computer systems, and possess Eva units without any provocation are now victims of human nature and Misato's personal bias.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:22 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:23 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: The reason we know that the Angels are bad is because they keep trying to kill everyone just to get back to their mommy

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:24 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: There is so much wrong with this that it's unbelievable. The reason we know that the Angels are bad is because they keep trying to kill everyone just to get back to their mommy; much like we know Gendo is bad because he's willing to do awful things just to get back to his Yui. I can't believe how crazy some of the perceptions about the series and its characters are here at this forum from some folks some times.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:27 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: The reason we know that the Angels are bad is because they keep trying to kill everyone just to get back to their mommy

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:31 pm In essence, the humans tried to imprison the Angel God because Lilith was all drained up and useless. By doing so, they unleashed the innocent Angels upon a world they were unprepared for and turned Adam into nothing more than a helpless fetus. And yet Kaworu still loves the little human bastards enough to give them one more chance at redemption, which once again makes his status as a Christ figure more solid. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:42 pm I don't quite get the bitter sarcasm at work here. I had to delete one post already. Let's keep it cool, folks. Grimmjow wrote: In essence, the humans tried to imprison the Angel God because Lilith was all drained up and useless.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:48 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:51 pm Reichu wrote: I don't quite get the bitter sarcasm at work here. I had to delete one post already. Let's keep it cool, folks. Is that the actual reason...? The correlation is kind of rough. The Angels were probably still around in soul form, and Kaworu was their last hope for reclaiming their future, but he betrayed them in favor of the species that raped Adam, butchered the Angels, and was making plans to kill itself and all life on Earth. So, yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 10:07 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: First off, I think I've succumbed to a misconception that was ongoing in this thread... the Angels aren't coming for they're mommy, they're coming for someone else's mom... Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Mon Apr 11, 2011 10:36 pm Oh yeah because Sahaquiel really just needed a good talking to. At best the angels were creatures working on pure instinct to get to Lilith. And at worst, seemingly random creatures of destruction (see Sahaquiel).

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Apr 11, 2011 10:45 pm The Lilin already established a sort of precedent before the Angels were even born -- instead of treating Adam like a human being, and attempting to make reconciliations for the wrongs done to her by Lilith's arrival, Adam was, essentially, raped and destroyed.
